title: The Aurora of the Philosophers
subtitle: Paracelsus on the Origin, Matter, and Preparation of the Philosophers' Stone — the Divine Ternary and the Supercelestial Marriage

summary.one_line: Paracelsus' systematic treatise on the Philosophers' Stone, covering its origin from Adam through the Chaldeans and Egyptians, the errors of the sophists, and the true preparation through vitriol, ...
summary.scope: Covers chapters I-VIII and XII-XX of Paracelsus' Aurora: the origin of the Art from Adam's hieroglyphic tables, the Egyptian priesthood tradition, the Ternary and Quaternary, the errors of seeking in vegetables/animals/minerals, the vitriol arcanum, the antimony bath, the universal matter, the secret fire, and the supercelestial marriage of soul and body.

sources.label: Paracelsus — The Aurora of the Philosophers (1659 English edition, transcribed at alchemywebsite.com)

body.text: ADAM was the first inventor of arts, because he had knowledge of all things as well after the Fall as before. Thence he predicted the world's destruction by water. From this cause, too, it came about that his successors erected two tables of stone, on which they engraved all natural arts in hieroglyphical characters, in order that their posterity might also become acquainted with this prediction. Subsequently, Noah found one of these tables under Mount Araroth, after the Deluge. In this table were described the courses of the upper firmament and of the lower globe, and also of the planets. At length this universal knowledge was divided into several parts, and lessened in its vigour and power. By means of this separation, one man became an astronomer, another a magician, another a cabalist, and a fourth an alchemist.

Magic, indeed, is an art and faculty whereby the elementary bodies, their fruits, properties, virtues, and hidden operations are comprehended. But the cabala, by a subtle understanding of the Scriptures, seems to trace out the way to God for men ... Magic had its origin in the Divine Ternary and arose from the Trinity of God. For God marked all His creatures with this Ternary and engraved its hieroglyph on them with His own finger. ... This covenant of the Divine Ternary, diffused throughout the whole substance of things, is indissoluble. By this, also, we have the secrets of all Nature from the four elements. For the Ternary, with the magical Quaternary, produces a perfect Septenary. When the Quaternary rests in the Ternary, then arises the Light of the World on the horizon of eternity ... Then when the Quaternary and the Ternary mount to the Denary is accomplished their retrogression or reduction to unity.

There follows the greatest arcanum, that is to say, the Supercelestial Marriage of the Soul, consummately prepared and washed by the blood of the lamb, with its own splendid, shining, and purified body. This is the true supercelestial marriage by which life is prolonged to the last and predestined day. In this way, then, the soul and spirit of the Vitriol, which are its blood, are joined with its purified body, that they may be for eternity inseparable. Take, therefore, this our foliated earth in a glass phial. Into it pour gradually its own oil. The body will receive and embrace its soul; since the body is affected with extreme desire for the soul, and the soul is most perfectly delighted with the embrace of the body. Place this conjunction in a furnace of arcana, and keep it there for forty days.

This is a well-known sententious saying of the philosophers, 'Let fire and Azoc suffice thee'. Fire alone is the whole work and the entire art. ... Almadir says that the invisible rays of our fire of themselves suffice. ... 'Make a fire, vaporous, digesting, as for cooking, continuous, but not volatile or boiling, enclosed, shut off from the air, not burning, but altering and penetrating.' ... Ours is a corrosive fire, which brings over our vessel an air like a cloud, in which cloud the rays of this fire are hidden. ... this is the matter of our fire, namely, that it be kindled by the quiet spirit of sensible fire, which drives upwards, as it were, the heated chaos from the opposite quarter, and above our philosophic matter.

Paracelsus' account of Adam's hieroglyphic tables — the two stone tablets engraved with all natural arts — is Steiner's description of the Akashic Record, the imperishable memory of the cosmos that the initiate learns to read. The two tablets are the two pillars of the Kabbalah (Jachin and Boaz), the two hemispheres of the brain, and the double aspect of the microcosm/macrocosm. The fact that Noah 'found one of these tables under Mount Araroth' after the Flood indicates the survival of Atlantean wisdom into the post-diluvian period. In Steiner's account, the Atlantean initiates engraved their knowledge on imperishable materials before the Flood, preserving the primordial wisdom for later epochs. The 'division into several parts' (astronomy, magic, cabala, alchemy) is the fragmentation of the original unified wisdom that Steiner calls the 'Ur-Wissen' — the original knowledge that must be reunited in the spiritual-scientific age.

Paracelsus' number-sequence Ternary (3), Quaternary (4), Septenary (7), Denary (10), Reduction to Unity (1) — is the exact schema of the Śaiva-tattvas in Tantraloka. The Ternary is śakti (the triple power of icchā, jñāna, kriyā — will, knowledge, action). The Quaternary is the four pāśas (bonds): māyā, karma, mala, and the māyīya-veśa (the covering of illusion). The Septenary is the seven cakras. The Denary is the ten avadhūtas (states beyond the cakras in the sahasrāra) or the ten mahāvidyās. The 'reduction to unity' is the non-dual realization (Śiva-Śakti aikya) described in Tantraloka I.5-10: the return of the created order into the pure light of prakāśa-vimarśa. Paracelsus' statement that 'Light of the World arises on the horizon of eternity when the Quaternary rests in the Ternary' is the merging of the four elements (body) into the three principles (sulfur, mercury, salt) — the alchemical parallel to the tantric laya (dissolution) of the bhūtas into the tanmātras.

The Supercelestial Marriage — where the 'soul and spirit of the Vitriol are joined with its purified body, that they may be for eternity inseparable' — is the final stage of the HGA contact in the Abramelin operation and in Frater Acher's four-stage path. The 'vitriol' (from Visita Interiora Terrae Rectificando Invenies Occultum Lapidem) is the practitioner's own substance that has been purified through the Saturn retreat. The body 'affected with extreme desire for the soul' is the lower self's longing for the HGA; the soul 'delighted with the embrace of the body' is the HGA's descent into incarnation. The forty days in the 'furnace of arcana' is the 40-day/40-night retreat pattern found in Abramelin (the three-day HGA contact at the end of the 18-month preparation), Moses on Sinai (40 days), Christ in the wilderness (40 days), and the PGM's ritual purification period. The 'oil' that is poured onto the foliated earth is the spiritual unction (the chrism of the daimonic presence) that saturates the purified body and makes it incorruptible.

Paracelsus' secret fire — 'vaporous, digesting, continuous, enclosed, not burning but altering and penetrating' — is an exact description of prāṇāgni (the fire of prāṇa) in Layayoga. This fire is not the physical fire of the athanor but the internal fire of the breath that 'alter and penetrate' the subtle body. The 'invisible rays' are the subtle currents of prāṇa that flow through the nāḍīs. The fire that is 'kindled by the quiet spirit of sensible fire, which drives upward the heated chaos' is the kuṇḍalinī that is awakened by the silent repetition of the mantra (ajapā-japa) combined with breath control. The 'cloud' in which the rays are hidden is the śuṣka-svāsa (the dry breath) of the prāṇāyāma practice that creates the internal mist (the 'alchemical cloud') in which the transformation occurs. The 'corrosive fire' that brings over the vessel 'an air like a cloud' is the fire element (agni-tattva) of the maṇipūra-cakra, which digests the subtle impressions (vāsanās) and turns them into the 'chaos' that must be reordered. The continuous, non-boiling heat is the madhyama-prāṇa (the middle breath) that sustains the sadhana without interruption.

gaps: Chapters II-IV on Greek/Egyptian transmission not fully excerpted
The antimony bath and arsenic arcanum (XIV-XV) not yet excerpted
Full projection method and multiplication not yet covered

issues.title: Aurora authorship debated
issues.description: Some scholars suspect the Aurora may have been elaborated by Gerard Dorne, the editor, rather than being wholly by Paracelsus. The work is cited in other Paracelsus texts but not included in the 1658 Geneva folio.

issues.title: Chapters IX-XI on mineral and animal errors not excerpted
issues.description: Paracelsus' systematic refutation of false paths in minerals, animals, and vegetables contains important clarifications of what the Stone is NOT.

versions.summary: Initial RO covering Paracelsus' Aurora: Adam's hieroglyphic tables, magic/cabala/ternary, vitriol supercelestial marriage, and secret fire.

timeline.event: RO created in draft state from Paracelsus Aurora source
